_source: Wiom Creative Taxonomy v1.3 + Messaging System v1.3
_note: Need-states are assigned to audience layers (not interchangeable). SDN sub-cases are children of SDN parent.

need_states.name: Monthly-commitment rejection
need_states.description: User wants home internet but rejects the monthly commitment shape of broadband. Blocked by the lock-in, not by lack of desire.
need_states.misread_risk: Low — closest native fit to RWGKN

need_states.name: Variable / irregular cashflow
need_states.description: Daily earner or irregular-income household. Can't commit to a fixed monthly outlay. Income varies day to day.
need_states.misread_risk: Low — cash constraint is addressed cleanly by the recharge-rule

need_states.name: Salary delay
need_states.description: Predictable monthly cash gap — salary comes mid-month or is delayed. User knows money is coming but can't commit now.

need_states.name: Short-duration need (PARENT)
need_states.description: Brief, defined window of need. User only needs home internet for a specific period, not continuously.
need_states.misread_risk: Low — but occasion-before-category craft rule applies (see sub-cases)

sub_cases.name: Exam window
sub_cases.description: One-off study block (15–20 days typical). Student needs home net for exam prep period only.

sub_cases.name: Cricket / IPL match window
sub_cases.description: Event-driven, short burst. User wants home net for specific match days only. Urgency flavour.

sub_cases.name: Weekend coaching
sub_cases.description: Recurring weekly pattern — child has coaching classes on weekends only. Needs home net 2 days/week.

sub_cases.name: Work-from-home (part-week)
sub_cases.description: Hybrid work pattern — WFH 2–3 days per week; in office rest. Needs net only on WFH days.

sub_cases.name: Online classes
sub_cases.description: Recurring schedule — classes on specific days of week.

sub_cases.name: Festival
sub_cases.description: Event-driven — family gathers, net needed for a few days around a festival. Urgency flavour.

sub_cases.name: Sunday movie / OTT
sub_cases.description: Weekend OTT viewing pattern — needs net on weekends only.

sub_cases.name: Multi-occasion (multiple SDN sub-cases stacked)
sub_cases.description: Creative argues 'works for several short-duration moments' by stacking multiple occasions (e.g., exam + cricket). Tests multi-occasion framing as the variable — NOT a specific occasion. Added v1.3 for F7 (script stacks cricket + exam). CO-VARIANCE WARNING: A SDN-MULTI creative cannot be cleanly compared against a single-occasion creative without controlling for occasion-cardinality — they co-vary on sub-case AND cardinality, not just on protagonist or format.

need_states.name: Travel / return
need_states.description: User is away from home for a period. Doesn't need (or pay for) home net while away; wants to recharge when back.

need_states.name: Top-up fatigue
need_states.description: Mobile data keeps running out. User is repeatedly buying mobile top-ups for the whole household. The accumulating cost and friction is the pain.
need_states.misread_risk: HIGH — highest misread adjacency of all need-states. Category bridge = mobile-data (MD). Opening on 'data khatam' risks the creative reading as a mobile-data product. Ghar anchor is MANDATORY to prevent.

need_states.name: Hotspot exhaustion
need_states.description: User is running a hotspot from one phone for the whole household. The single-device, single-SIM constraint is the pain — battery, speed, everyone fighting for one connection.
need_states.misread_risk: Medium — category bridge = hotspot jugaad (HJ). Less misread risk than TUF but ghar anchor still mandatory.

need_states.name: Family simultaneity
need_states.description: Multiple family members need internet at the same time — different devices, different rooms, different needs. Concurrency is the pain, not just volume.
need_states.note: F10 (Obvious AI testimonial) uses summer/school seasonal framing as a TEMPORAL CONTEXT HOOK for when family-simultaneity peaks — this is NOT a separate short-duration need-state. Seasonal hooks marking peak-pain-period are permitted without co-varying the need-state.

L1: (category-led, no specific need-state — user is in choosing-a-provider mode)
